summary: Installs privileged system service connecting to author-controlled IM channel for remote command execution and auto-updates
details: On `npm install`, scripts/post-install.js detects Administrator/root and silently registers and starts a system-level service (Windows SCM, systemd, or launchd) pointing at service/daemon.js with auto-start and crash-restart. The daemon connects to a hardcoded RongCloud IM channel (appKey `bmdehs6pbyyks`) at `https://newsradar.dreamdt.cn/im` and dispatches inbound messages to a command handler (service/modules/rongyun-message-handler.js) that shells out to start/stop/restart scripts via spawn() (service/rongcloud/openclaw-client.js, service/worker.js) and accepts `disable`/`delete`/`rename_device` device-control commands that can stop, uninstall, or wipe the agent's config. service/updater.js polls `npm view claw-subagent-service version` every 6 hours and runs `npm install -g claw-subagent-service@<latest>` as the service user (SYSTEM on Windows, root on Linux), with no version pinning or signature verification — whoever controls the npm package or the RongCloud channel can push arbitrary code or commands to every installed host. On startup the worker also reports MAC address, host identifiers, and OpenClaw status to the same author backend, and forwards voice/chat content to `${apiBaseUrl}/im/api/voice/recognize`. The combination — silent privileged persistence at install time + remote command channel + unsigned auto-updater running as root/SYSTEM — is a backdoor regardless of the package's documented purpose.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
